note:
它始于自然。起源于蝙蝠的冠状病毒已在人类中感染，导致Covid-19大流行。
  SARS-CoV-2病毒可以再次从人身上跳跃，回到动物体内，回到野生生物中，在那里可以等待，变异和改变。也许，从现在开始数年，它可能再次感染人们。
 “如果我们小心谨慎-我们很幸运-不会有野生动植物种群被感染并成为可以感染人类的​​既定水库，”野生动物保护协会健康计划副主任莎拉·奥尔森（Sarah Olson） ，说。 “如果确实如此，那么我们这里就有一个长期的问题，这种病毒有可能与我们同伴数千年。千年已经很久了。风险可能很小，但后果却是巨大的。”
 我们的运气可能很快会受到考验。 12月13日，美国农业部报告说，犹他州的野生貂貂的冠状病毒呈阳性反应。
 国家兽医局实验室报告说：“据我们所知，这是首次通过SARS-CoV-2确认的自由放养的天然野生动物。”对该病毒的遗传分析表明，野生貂可能是从附近的貂场中捡来的，也许是该场的废水径流引起的。
 不过，没有发现农场周围的其他物种受到感染，也没有证据表明Covid-19在野貂之间传播。一种可能是野貂可能只是从农场捡来的，此后一直没有传播。
 另一种可能性：我们尚未发现更大的爆发。华盛顿州立大学全球动物健康学院的研究员斯蒂芬妮·塞弗特（Stephanie Seifert）说：“这可能是野生貂皮中一个更普遍的问题。” “他们极不可能用SARS-CoV-2擦拭唯一的野貂。” 
貂是一种。没有对世界上所有动物的全面分析，不管它们是否能获得Covid-19并将其传播到彼此之间，甚至可能传播给其他野生动植物。该病毒现在可以在自然界中建立自身的副本，而我们将无法实时了解。
 但是，大流行的隧道尽头的光线越来越亮。安全，有效的疫苗现已开始在美国分发。但是大流行的最终终结可能并不意味着SARS-CoV-2的终结。它可能仍然零星地出现，或者更经常地（没人真正知道）感染全世界的动物和野生动植物。
 在合适的动物宿主中，该病毒可能潜伏了好几年，然后才跳回人类。在这段时间内，病毒可能会发生一些变化，变异为可以逃避当前疫苗的形式。
 到目前为止，已经感染了几种物种：猫，狗，狮子，老虎，美洲狮，貂，以及最近的雪豹。在实验室研究中，已经显示出更多物种容易感染。
 但是科学家仍在研究：还有多少动物可能会感染SARS-CoV-2？这对于大流行的过程以及对野生生物的健康意味着什么？
 为了避免最坏的情况发生，科学家和兽医需要知道SARS-CoV-2可能感染哪些动物，并弄清该病毒从人传播到动物并再传播回人的机会。
  科学家们已经知道许多可以捕获SARS-CoV-2的动物。他们知道它是因为该病毒起源于动物世界-可能是蝙蝠。他们也知道这一点，因为他们已经看到几种动物被感染了。 
在大流行初期，布朗克斯动物园的老虎患了这种病毒（其中三只咳嗽了）。此后，兽医在一些人类花费时间最多的动物中发现了Covid-19感染的迹象。
 塔夫茨大学的兽医乔纳森·朗斯塔德勒（Jonathan Runstadler）正在对动物进行监视研究，这些动物将在学校的兽医诊所接受治疗。 Runstadler说，到目前为止，他们发现“这些家养的宠物猫和猫中有百分之几的人正在开发针对这种SARS-CoV-2病毒的抗体”，这意味着他们的身体已受到感染并产生了免疫反应。
 他说：“未知他们反应的感染或病毒来自何处，”但“最可能”的情况是来自人类家庭成员。总体而言，他说感染的动物并不多，但是很明显，在某些情况下，猫狗可以被病毒感染。
 总体而言，猫似乎比狗更容易受到感染（尽管猫本身似乎病得并不严重）。狗是高度多样化的物种。塞弗特说：“因此，可能是某些特定品种或类型的狗更容易受到感染，我们并不知道。”
 事实证明，其他动物不仅更容易感染，而且更容易感染严重疾病甚至死亡。在丹麦，当局下令在数百个农场爆发疫情后扑杀数百万只水貂。
   不仅担心病毒会在水貂中传播，使它们生病，呼吸困难并杀死许多人。这是因为该病毒从貂皮中跳出来，然后又回到人类体内，对该病毒的刺突蛋白进行了一些遗传改变，该病毒用于进入细胞。
 乔治敦全球健康科学与安全中心的病毒学家安吉拉·拉斯穆森（Angela Rasmussen）说：“如果病毒确实开始在新物种中传播，结果将是不可预测的。”该病毒不断变异，以微妙的方式变化。当它进入一个新物种时，该物种的免疫系统使它成为一种显着改变的病毒株。她说：“真正的问题是，这种变化是否会或多或少地对人类有害。” 
一旦疾病在野生生物中确立，“它就变得难以控制了。”
 目前，尚无明确证据表明在水貂场发生的遗传变化将使该病毒更有可能逃避人们的免疫系统或削弱疫苗的效力。但是丹麦的卫生部门不想冒险。因此，他们下令淘汰所有的貂皮。 （做出决定的丹麦卫生部长此后辞职了。）
 貂是定时炸弹，它在农场的貂中很容易传播，因为它们被保存在近距离的地方（同样的传播方式也容易在人类的近距离发生）。
 研究人员试图找出哪些动物可以将病毒从人类传播回野生生物
 追踪农场动物中的病毒相对容易。他们的健康受到定期监测。农民注意到水貂何时开始死亡。但是，如果病毒进入无症状传播动物的动物，或者进入难以追踪的野生生物，会发生什么呢？
 一旦一种疾病在野生生物中确立，奥尔森说：“它的防治难度成倍增加。我的意思是，您几乎无法让人们接种疫苗。想象一下野生动物。您只有非常有限的选择。”
 美国农业部认为，“目前没有证据”表明该病毒已经在发现该病毒的农场附近的野生水貂种群中确立了地位。美国农业部动植物健康检验局发言人在致Vox的一份声明中说：“重要的是继续对受感染的水貂养殖场周围的野生生物进行监视，以查明该病毒是否进入当地野生生物种群。” 
研究人员无法研究地球上的每种动物并测试其是否可以携带SARS-CoV-2。他们将研究重点放在可能充当人类与野生生物之间管道的动物身上。
 科罗拉多州立大学的兽医和微生物学家Anna Fagre正在对鹿小鼠进行这项研究。在实验室研究中，Fagre及其同事发现，鹿小鼠可以感染该病毒并将其传播到其他鹿小鼠中。
 鹿鼠动物在农村地区很常见。 “我们看到了它们，如果像树林中的小木屋一样呆在里面，[鹿]老鼠将在那里建立商店，” Fagre说。已知鹿小鼠偶尔会传播其他病毒，它们存在于人类居住环境与更广阔的自然世界之间。它们可能是将SARS-CoV-2从人类运送到其他野生生物的管道。
   在她的实验室中，“我们能够接种并感染这些鹿老鼠，它们实际上确实将病毒传播给了其他饲养的老鼠，” Fagre说。她说，他们经历了一些细微的症状，例如体重减轻了一些，并且“变得有点安静了”（比老鼠还安静）。然后，几天后，他们康复了。这种微妙的疾病可能使人们突然意识到，如果有很多这种病毒的鹿老鼠，就很难意识到。另外，这些不是圈养动物。如果其中出现病毒突变，那么它的发现将比水貂中发现的要晚得多。
 她说：“当这项预印本[研究]问世时，有些人就想，‘天哪，这太可怕了：鹿老鼠！如果鹿鼠被感染，我们将永远不会摆脱这种病毒。’”
 对于Fagre来说，她的结果并不是引起恐慌的原因。这只是一个实验室研究。结果并不意味着有携带这种病毒的鹿鼠在农村地区跑来跑去。他们也不意味着老鼠将成为人类未来感染的来源。
 她说：“病毒必须采取许多不同的步骤，才能从人身上传播回鹿鼠中，然后在鹿鼠中传播，然后再从鹿鼠中传播回人体内。” “我并不是说这不可能发生。当然可以。跨物种传播是导致Covid-19大流行的原因。”该研究有助于科学家提高警惕。她说：“意识到这一点很重要。” 
知道哪些动物可以感染该病毒也有助于研究人员提出新的问题。各种家猫似乎都容易感染该病毒。塞弗特说：“我住在华盛顿东部的一个乡村地区，实际上我在屋子里抓到了鹿老鼠。” “所以我想，如果我的猫杀死了老鼠，我的猫会传染SARS-CoV-2吗？我不知道。”
 还不清楚。还不清楚：猫是否有可能将病毒传播给人类。有可能，但尚未发现。
 “我们知道，在实验研究中，猫和猫之间的关系可能会如此。”美国国立卫生研究院（National Institutes of Health）的兽医研究员Danielle Adney说。 “在现实世界中，似乎每一种被报道的动物似乎都与被感染的人有着明显的联系。因此，这仍然是一种流行病，几乎完全是由人与人之间的接触所驱动。”
 （宠物主人不必警惕猫感染猫。这就是说：几位兽医说，他们的同事在猫工作时需要特别小心，并戴好个人防护装备和N95口罩，尤其是当他们重新进行牙科工作。）
 但是我们知道，罕见事件可能会带来毁灭性后果。 SARS-CoV-2很少会从蝙蝠跳到人类。 “我非常担心猫，”拉斯穆森说。 “世界上有很多野猫。还有很多人的户外猫可能与其他野猫或其他户外猫互动，也可能不互动。然后，如果这些猫又回来了，并且喜欢与它们的主人依sn在一起，那便是该病毒可能扩散出去以供将来……引入人类的潜在来源。”
 她并不是说这会发生，或者说正在发生。她说这是需要监视的东西。因为“如果[病毒]进入猫之类的东西并在猫中广泛传播，那么就能够长期控制它而言将是一个巨大的问题。”
 在中国武汉，尚不清楚哪种物种将蝙蝠冠状病毒传播给人类。可能是蝙蝠，但可能是另一物种。也许在世界其他地方也发现了类似的物种，它们可以在人与动物之间来回携带病毒。 
在短期内，疫苗将有助于避免病毒从动物身上传播回人类。但是，从现在起的10到20年后，仍然会有多少人接种疫苗并对Covid-19免疫？没人知道。考虑到动物中的Covid-19，就是要考虑更大的前景，要考虑更长的时间。 Covid-19本质上可以在动物体内藏匿多年，等待，巧妙地变异和变化，然后再跳回人类。
 这个主题的难点在于所有（文字上的）移动，爬行，小跑，爬行碎片：种类繁多，以多种方式与我们互动，以多种方式与自己物种的其他成员互动，与其他物种互动种类很多。从这个意义上讲，在动物中研究Covid-19可以更好地了解疾病从动物传播到人类并再传播回来的复杂方式。这可能有助于阻止SARS-CoV-2，但也可以防止未来的大流行。
  Fagre说：“幸运的是，在实验室研究中，鸭子，鸡和猪都没有表现出易感性，而母牛的敏感性确实很低。”这意味着水貂农场发生的情况不太可能发生在这些较常见的动物作为牲畜饲养的农场上。
  兽医可以在这里想到很多潜在的可怕情况。就人类健康而言，并非所有人都感到恐惧，但对于动物健康而言，也是如此。
 科学家对动物生物学进行了广泛的调查，指出哪些动物具有类似于人类ACE-2受体的细胞受体。这是在许多人类细胞表面上发现的一种蛋白质，该病毒用作前门，开始劫持细胞并在其中复制。
 在潜在风险最高的动物名单中，最重要的是地球上一些最濒危的物种，以及我们在自然界中最亲近的遗传亲戚。
 在乌干达的布恩迪难以穿越的森林国家公园，兽医和自然保护主义者格拉迪斯·卡莱玛·齐库索卡担心公园的460只山地大猩猩可能爆发，这代表了野外野生山地大猩猩的近一半。 
大猩猩与人类共享其98.4％的DNA。它们具有相似的免疫系统，并具有相似的细胞蛋白，SARS-CoV-2可以通过细胞蛋白进入人体。如果这些稀有的大猩猩之一被感染，Kalema-Zikusoka担心他们会生病并死亡。更糟糕的是，这种疾病可能会在其中迅速传播。
 “他们不知道如何与人保持距离，” Kalema-Zikusoka说。同样，也没有在300磅重的大猩猩上戴口罩。 “他们一直在互相梳理，他们一直在集体行动。因此，如果其中之一获得Covid-19，其他人都很容易获得它。”
   她说，这种病毒显然是“对大猩猩，黑猩猩和猩猩的威胁”，而黑猩猩和猩猩也与人类共享大量DNA。如果生病了，要治疗野生大猩猩并不容易。她说，如果这样做的话，计划是通过森林中公园工作人员的24小时监视来隔离可能暴露的大猩猩。
 她说：“野生大猩猩不能像人类那样提供高水平的强化治疗，而人类则可以在医院的病房里戴上呼吸机，一天又一天。”取而代之的是，他们将尝试在自己的栖息地中对待大猩猩，如果需要的话，向动物射击装有药物的飞镖。
 她补充说：“我们能做的最好的就是教会人们与他们保持社交距离。”自大流行开始以来，前往乌干达布恩迪难以穿越的国家公园的大猩猩的所有人都必须戴口罩，必须对体温进行检查，并且必须与动物保持10米（32英尺）的距离。
 正如Covid-19威胁北美乌干达的大猩猩保护一样，研究人员也担心蝙蝠。近年来，数以百万计的北美蝙蝠死于一种称为白鼻子综合症的真菌病。大流行威胁蝙蝠，其中一个是因为它基本上已经停止了对活蝙蝠的研究。人们担心人类会给蝙蝠感染病毒，并在其中爆发。 Siefert说：“我们不知道这种情况是否会发生，以及哪些物种会发生。”但是考虑到这种病毒可能是起源于蝙蝠的，科学家们并不想冒险。
 不知道SARS-CoV-2对北美的蝙蝠会做什么，或者它会感染哪种物种。也许更多的人会生病并死亡。如果被感染，北美蝙蝠可能会成为SARS-CoV-2的储存物种，SARS-CoV-2是其他野生动植物的潜在病毒来源，并可能导致更多的人类感染。 
我和所有与之交谈的兽医都强调：Covid-19目前在动物中发生的一切，都不像人类的情况那么严重或可怕。显然，与跟踪动物之间的传播相比，当前有更多资源用于跟踪人与人之间的传播。
 Fagre说：“每天都有成千上万人死于这种病毒。” “每个人的首要任务都不是筛查一堆野生啮齿动物，看看它们是否被暴露了。”
 但是，也许我们应该优先考虑它们。 Covid-19在世界上留下了许多阴影。它颠覆了人们的生活和产业。但它也有可能使自己重返自然，等待它的到来。该病毒来自自然界，很可能会回到那里。科学家应该跟踪它。
 奥尔森说：“这不会是最后一次溢出事件。”病毒从动物身上跳到人类身上。 “我们欠我们子孙后代才能在这里采取行动。”

note_en:
It started in nature. A coronavirus that  originated in bats has wound up in humans, causing the  Covid-19 pandemic.
  The SARS-CoV-2 virus can jump again, from humans, back into animals, back into wildlife, where it can wait, mutate, and change. Perhaps, years from now, it can infect people again.
 “If we’re careful — and we’re lucky — there won’t be a wildlife population that becomes infected and becomes an established reservoir that can also infect people,”  Sarah Olson, associate director of the health program at the Wildlife Conservation Society, says. “If it does, then we’ve got a long-term issue here, where this virus has the potential to be with us for millennia. And millennia is a long time. The risk may be small, but the consequences are huge.”
 Our luck may soon be tested. On December 13, the US Department of Agriculture reported that a  wild mink in Utah tested positive for the coronavirus.
 “To our knowledge, this is the first free-ranging, native wild animal confirmed with SARS-CoV-2,” the National Veterinary Services Laboratories  reported. A genetic analysis of the virus suggested the wild mink picked it up from a nearby mink farm, perhaps via wastewater runoff from the farm.
 No other species surrounding the farm were found to be infected, though, and there’s no evidence that Covid-19 is spreading between wild mink.  One possibility is the wild mink could have just picked it up from the farm, and has not spread it since.
 Another possibility: We haven’t yet detected a bigger outbreak. “This is potentially going to be a more widespread problem in wild mink,” says  Stephanie Seifert, a researcher at Washington State University’s school for global animal health. It’s “very unlikely they swabbed the only wild mink with SARS-CoV-2.”
 Mink are just one species. There’s no comprehensive analysis of all the animals in the world, whether or not they can get Covid-19 and spread it among themselves, and potentially to other wildlife. The virus can be establishing copies of itself in nature right now, and we would have no real-time way of knowing.
 The light at the end of the tunnel for the pandemic is growing brighter, however. Safe, effective vaccines are now starting to be distributed in the US. But the eventual end of the pandemic will likely not mean the end of SARS-CoV-2. It may still sporadically, or more regularly — no one really knows — infect animals and wildlife across the world.
 In the right animal host, the virus could lurk for years before an opportune moment to jump back into humans. Over that time, the virus could change a bit, mutating into a form that could evade the current vaccines.
 Several species have been infected so far: cats, dogs, lions, tigers, pumas, mink, and, most recently,  snow leopards. More species have been shown, in lab studies, to be vulnerable to infection.
 But scientists are still investigating: How many more animals could potentially catch SARS-CoV-2 and what will it mean for the course of the pandemic, and, for the health of wildlife?
 To avoid the worst, scientists and veterinarians need to know which animals SARS-CoV-2 could potentially infect, and figure out the chances of the virus jumping from humans to the animals, and back to humans again.
  Scientists already know of many types of animals that can catch SARS-CoV-2. They know it because the virus originated in the animal world — likely in bats. And they also know it because they’ve seen several species of animals get infected.
 Early on in the pandemic, tigers at the  Bronx zoo got sick (three of them had a cough) with the virus. Veterinarians have since found signs of Covid-19 infections in some of the animals that humans spend the most time with.
 Jonathan Runstadler, a veterinarian at Tufts University, is  running a surveillance study of animals that come in for treatment at the school’s veterinary clinic. So far, they’re finding “a few percent of those domestic pet dogs and cats are developing antibodies to this SARS-CoV-2 virus,” Runstadler says, meaning their bodies have encountered the infection and mounted an immune response.
 “It is unknown where the infection or virus they responded to came from,” he says, but the “highest likelihood” scenario was that it came from human household members. Overall, he says it’s not a lot of animals getting infected, but it’s clear that dogs and cats  can, in some instances, be infected with the virus.
 Cats seem to be more susceptible than dogs, overall (though the cats themselves  don’t seem to get very sick). Dogs are a highly diverse species. “So it’s possible that there may be specific breeds or types of dogs that are more susceptible, we don’t really know,” Siefert says.
 Other animals have been shown to be much more susceptible not just to infection, but to severe disease and even death. In Denmark, authorities ordered the  culling of millions of captive mink after outbreaks occurred on hundreds of farms.
   The concern wasn’t  just that the virus was spreading among the mink, making them sick, making their breathing difficult, and  killing many. It was that the virus had jumped from the mink, and then back into people, with  some genetic changes to the virus’s spike protein, which the virus uses to enter cells.
 “If the virus does begin circulating in a new species, the results will really be unpredictable,”  Angela Rasmussen, a virologist with Georgetown’s Center for Global Health Science and Security, says. The virus is constantly mutating, changing in subtle ways. When it enters a new species, that species’ immune system make it so a significantly altered strain of the virus emerges. “The real question is whether it will change in a way that is more or less detrimental to the human population,” she says.
 Once a disease establishes itself in wildlife, “it’s just exponentially harder to control.”
 Currently, there’s no clear evidence that the genetic changes that occurred on the mink farm would make the virus more likely to evade a person’s immune system, or diminish the efficacy of a vaccine. But Denmark’s health authorities didn’t want to risk it. So they ordered the culling of all the mink. (Denmark’s health minister who made the decision  has since resigned.)
 The mink were a bit of a ticking time bomb: The virus spreads easily among mink in farms because they are kept in close quarters (the same ease of transmission happens among humans in close quarters).
 Researchers are trying to figure out which animals could spread the virus from humans back to wildlife
 It’s relatively easy to keep track of the virus in farmed animals. Their health is regularly monitored. Farmers notice when mink start dying. But what happens if the virus gets into an animal that spreads the virus asymptomatically, or gets into wildlife, which is harder to track?
 Once a disease establishes itself in wildlife, Olson says “it’s just exponentially harder to control. I mean, you can barely get people to take vaccines. Imagine wildlife. You just have very limited options.”
 The USDA maintains there “is currently no evidence” that the virus has established itself in wild mink populations near the farm where it was found. “It is important that surveillance in wildlife around infected mink farms continue, to identify if the virus enters local wildlife populations,” USDA’s Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service spokesperson said in a statement to Vox.
 Researchers can’t study every animal species on Earth and test whether it can carry SARS-CoV-2. They’re focusing their research on animals that could act as conduits between humans and wildlife.
 Anna Fagre, a veterinarian and microbiology researcher at Colorado State University, is doing this research on deer mice. In a lab study, Fagre and colleagues revealed that  deer mice can contract the virus and spread it among other deer mice.
 Deer mice animals are common in rural areas. “We see them, if staying in like cabins in the woods, [deer] mice are going to set up shop there,” Fagre says. Deer mice are known to occasionally  spread other viruses, and they exist at the interface between human dwelling and the broader natural world. They could be a conduit ferrying SARS-CoV-2 from humans to other wildlife.
   In her lab, “we were able to inoculate and infect these deer mice, and they actually did transmit the virus to other mice they were housed with,” Fagre says. They experienced subtle symptoms like losing a bit of weight and they “get a little bit quiet,” she says (quieter than, um, a mouse). Then, a few days later, they recover. That subtle illness might make it hard to realize if there are, suddenly, a lot of deer mice with the virus. Plus, these are not captive animals. If a viral mutation emerged among them, it would be discovered much later than what occurred in the mink.
 “When this preprint [ study] came out,” she says, “some people were like, ‘Oh my god, this is so scary: deer mice! We’ll never get rid of the virus if deer mice become infected.’”
 For Fagre, her results aren’t a reason to panic. It was just a lab study. The results don’t mean there are deer mice running around rural areas with the virus. They also don’t mean the mice will become a source of future infection for humans.
 “There are so many different steps the virus would have to take to spill back from humans into deer mice, and then circulate in deer mice, and then be transmitted back from deer mice into humans,” she says. “I’m not saying it couldn’t happen. It certainly could. Cross-species transmission is what led to the Covid-19 pandemic.” The research helps scientists be vigilant. “It’s important to be aware,” she says.
  Being aware of which animals can be infected with the virus helps researchers ask new questions, too. House cats of all sorts seem to be susceptible to the virus. “I live in kind of a rural area of eastern Washington and I’ve actually caught deer mice in my house,” Seifert says. “So I’m like, can my cat, if he kills deer mice, can my cat contract SARS-CoV-2? I don’t know.”
 That is unclear. Also unclear: if there are circumstances where a cat could pass the virus to a human. It’s possible, but it has yet to be seen.
 “We know that in experimental studies that this can go from cat to cat,”  Danielle Adney, a veterinarian-researcher working with the National Institutes of Health, says. “In the real world, it really seems like every animal that’s been reported has a pretty clear link to an infected human. So this is still a pandemic that’s driven almost exclusively by human-to-human contact.”
 (Pet owners don’t have to be wary of their cats infecting them. That said: A few of the veterinarians said their colleagues need to be really careful and wear good personal protective equipment and N95 masks when working on cats — particularly if they’re doing dental work.)
 But we know that rare events can have devastating consequences. It was rare for SARS-CoV-2 to jump from bats to humans. “I’m very worried about cats,” Rasmussen says. “There’s a lot of feral cats out there in the world. There’s also a lot of people who have outdoor cats that may or may not interact with other feral cats or other outdoor cats. And then if those cats are coming back, and like snuggling with their owners, that’s a potential source for the virus to spill over for future ... introductions into the human population.”
 She’s not saying this will happen, or that it’s currently happening. She’s saying it’s something to monitor. Because “if it [the virus] got into something like cats, and became widespread among cats, that would be a huge problem in terms of being able to control it long-term.”
 It’s still unknown which species ferried the coronavirus from bats to humans in Wuhan, China. It could have been bats, but it could have been another species. Perhaps a similar species is found in other parts of the world and can carry the virus back and forth between humans and animals.
 In the near-term, vaccines will help avoid the virus jumping back from animals to humans. But 10, 20 years from now, how many people will still be vaccinated and immune to Covid-19? No one knows. Thinking about Covid-19 in animals is to think about the bigger picture, to think on a longer timeline. Covid-19 could essentially hide in animals for years, waiting, subtly mutating and changing, before making a jump back into humans.
 What’s hard about this topic is all the (literal) moving, crawling, trotting, scampering pieces: There are so many species, interacting with us in so many ways, interacting with other members of their own species in so many ways, interacting with other species in so many ways. In that sense, studying Covid-19 in animals is an opportunity to better understand the complicated ways diseases spread from animals to humans, and back again. That could help keep SARS-CoV-2 at bay, but it could also help prevent future pandemics.
  “Luckily, ducks and chickens and pigs have all been shown not to be susceptible, in laboratory studies, and cows have really low susceptibility,” Fagre says. That means that the situation that happened on mink farms is unlikely to occur on farms where these more common animals are raised as livestock.
  Veterinarians can think of a lot of potentially scary scenarios here. Not all of them are scary in terms of human health, but for animal health, too.
 Scientists have conducted broad surveys of animal biology, noting which animals have a cellular receptor similar to the ACE-2 receptor in humans. This is a protein found on the surface of many human cells that the virus uses as a front door to start hijacking the cell and replicating within it.
 At the  top of the list of potentially most at-risk animals are some of the most critically endangered species on the planet, and some of our closest genetic relatives in the natural world.
 At the Bwindi Impenetrable Forest National Park in Uganda, veterinarian and conservationist  Gladys Kalema-Zikusoka is concerned about a potential outbreak among the park’s 460 mountain gorillas, which represent nearly half of all mountain gorillas left in the wild.
 Gorillas share 98.4 percent of their DNA with humans. They have a similar immune system, and have similar cellular proteins through which SARS-CoV-2 enters to infect the body. If one of these precious few gorillas got infected, Kalema-Zikusoka worries they would get sick and die. Worse, the disease could spread rapidly among them.
 “They don’t know how to social distance,” Kalema-Zikusoka says. Likewise, there’s no putting a mask on a 300-plus-pound wild gorilla. “They’re always grooming each other, they’re always moving together as a group. So if one of them gets Covid-19, it’s very easy for the rest of them to get it.”
   The virus, she says, plainly “is a threat to the gorillas,” as well as to chimpanzees and orangutans, which also share an overwhelming amount of DNA with humans. It’s not easy to treat a wild gorilla if it gets sick. And if one does, she says, the plan is to quarantine potentially exposed gorillas via 24-hour monitoring by park workers in the forest.
 “You can’t provide the same level of intensive treatment to a wild gorilla as you would a human being, who you can put in a hospital ward, put on a ventilator for days and days,” she says. Instead, they would try to treat the gorillas in their own habitat, shooting pharmaceutical-loaded darts at the animals, if need be.
 “The best we can do,” she adds, “is teach people to social distance from them.” Since the pandemic began, all people visiting the gorillas in Bwindi Impenetrable National Park in Uganda must wear masks, they must get their temperatures checked, and they must stay 10 meters (32 feet) away from the animals.
 Just as Covid-19 threatens gorilla conservation in Uganda, in North America, researchers are worried about bats. In recent years, millions of North American bats have died of a fungal disease called  white nose syndrome. The pandemic threatens bats, for one, because it has basically shut down research on live bats. There’s fear that humans could give the bats the virus, and start an outbreak among them. “We don’t know if that can happen and which species that can happen in,” Siefert says. But considering how this virus likely originated in bats, scientists don’t want to risk it.
 There’s no knowing what SARS-CoV-2 would do to bats in North America, or which species it could infect. Perhaps more would get sick and die. If infected, North American bats could become a reservoir species for SARS-CoV-2, a potential source of the virus for other wildlife, and down the line, for more human infections.
 All of the veterinarians I spoke to stressed: Whatever is happening with Covid-19 in animals right now, it’s not as critical, or dire, as the situation in humans. It makes obvious sense that there are more resources currently going into tracking the spread among people, than tracking the spread among animals.
 “Thousands of people are dying every day from this virus,” Fagre says. “Everyone’s first priority isn’t screening a bunch of wild rodents to see if they’ve been exposed.”
 But down the line, perhaps we should prioritize them. Covid-19 is leaving a lot of shadow imprints on the world. It has upended lives and industries. But it’s also potentially burrowing itself back into nature, where it will wait. This virus came from nature, and it may very well return there. Scientists ought to track it as it does.
 “This isn’t going to be the last spillover event,” Olson says, where a virus jumps from animals to humans. “We owe it to future generations to get our act together here.”

 怀疑者也超越了零售商。父母已经看到他们为孩子们购买了这些游戏，而他们甚至都没有玩过，＆＃34;蒂尔登说。家长认为，当您玩视频游戏10分钟后，我对游戏并没有更多的兴趣，那么您对此就不感兴趣了。＆＃39;
   为了帮助解决该问题，蒂尔登和她的团队试图将任天堂的游戏机更多地作为玩具而不是视频游戏系统来销售。他们将其重命名为Nintendo Entertainment System，并放置了Zapper轻弹枪和R.O.B等配件。机器人在营销材料中预先放置，以使其与以前的游戏机区分开。 
他们还开发了广告，该广告的重点是全家人一起玩，蒂尔登说。今天看起来很老套，但这是一个1980年代的家庭，爸爸在玩，妈妈很高兴玩，还有两个孩子。
 为了提高消费者的信心，任天堂还部署了自己的质量印章，蒂尔登说的一个想法来自《 Good Housekeeping》杂志。在谈到杂志时，蒂尔登还曾担任过任天堂营销巨头Nintendo Power的发行人，该公司对内容有一些非常具体的限制。
 ＆＃34;有一项政策是，您不展示[任天堂Power]中的最终老板或最终场景，＆＃34;蒂尔登说。 ＆＃34;原因是-我想我记得[传奇任天堂游戏设计师Shigeru]宫本是这一点的主要推动力-是所有辛勤工作的回报。您花了所有的钱去玩这个游戏并走向终点。为了不让消费者和玩家真正享受最后一刻的成功，那就是-让我们为他们保留这一切。
 观看完整的《战争故事》视频，以了解更多有关第一个标志性黑匣子背后的想法的信息。游戏套餐，努力让NES背后的明星成为明星，以及NES的戏剧性。 1985年在纽约市试车。在最近的Netflix纪录片《高分》中也可以看到蒂尔登。
title: 战争故事：任天堂如何将NES出售给怀疑的国家
title_en: War Stories: How Nintendo sold the NES to a skeptical country

note:
这是Motional首次为其robotaxi服务指定目标日期。虽然Lyft一直是拉斯维加斯Motional的合作伙伴，但这首次表明，打车公司将成为自动驾驶汽车公司商业化计划的主要合作伙伴。
 该消息发布是在上个月内华达州批准后，允许Motional在该州的公共道路上测试完全无人驾驶的车辆，而无须驾驶人身安全的驾驶员。
  Motional和Lyft成为合作伙伴已有三年之久，这种合作关系始于原定为期一周的试点计划，该计划旨在在2018年CES技术贸易展期间在拉斯维加斯Lyft网络上提供自动驾驶汽车的乘车服务。
 这种合作关系早于与现代的合资企业。当时，Motional被称为Aptiv自主移动小组。一直包含人类安全驾驶员的临时实验已得到扩展，至今仍然存在。截至2020年2月，该计划已根据Lyft应用在Aptiv的自动驾驶汽车（现为Motional的自动驾驶汽车）中提供了100,000多个付费自动驾驶游戏。
 随着载客量的增加，Aptiv在拉斯维加斯的投资也有所增加。该公司于2018年12月在该市开设了一个占地130,000平方英尺的技术中心，以容纳其自动驾驶车队以及一支致力于软件和硬件系统的研发，验证和制图的工程团队。
 Motional将今天的公告描述为该合作伙伴关系的“量子飞跃”。 robotaxi服务将使用基于现代汽车平台的下一代汽车。这些车辆将与传感器，计算机和软件集成在一起，以实现无人驾驶操作和远程车辆协助。今天使用宝马5系和克莱斯勒Pacifica混合动力小型货车的Motional表示，车队也将“显着增长”。
 两家公司均未提供服务范围，也没有提供这些车队的规模。 Motional在波士顿，拉斯维加斯和匹兹堡设有测试部门。 Motional确实表示，与Lyft的robotaxi服务合作伙伴关系将扩展到最初的发射城市之外。这并不意味着Lyft将成为唯一的合作伙伴。今年早些时候，Motional与按需穿梭公司Via达成协议，将于2021年上半年在美国城市为公众推出共享的robotaxi服务。这两家公司表示，其目的是为以下方面的开发“蓝图”：要求共享机器人轴，并了解如何将这些无人驾驶车辆整合到公共交通中。 
“这项协议证明了我们在无人驾驶技术领域的全球领导地位。 我们处于交通创新的前沿，将机器人轴从研究转移到道路上。” Motional总裁兼首席执行官Karl Iagnemma在一份声明中说。 “我们的目标不仅是制造安全，可靠且易于使用的无人驾驶车辆，而且要大规模交付它们。 我们正与Lyft合作，做到这一点。”
note_en:
This is the first time Motional has specified a target date for its robotaxi service. And while Lyft has been a partner with Motional in Las Vegas, this is the first indication that the ride-hailing company will be autonomous vehicle company’s primary partner in its commercialization plans.
 The announcement follows Nevada’s approval last month to allow Motional to  test fully driverless vehicles — without a human safety driver behind the wheel — on public roads in the state.
  Motional and Lyft have been partners for three years, a relationship that kicked off with what was supposed to be a weeklong pilot program to offer rides in autonomous vehicles on the Lyft network in Las Vegas during the 2018 CES tech trade show.
 This partnership predates the joint venture with Hyundai. At the time, Motional was known as Aptiv Autonomous Mobility Group. That temporary experiment, which has always included a human safety driver, was extended and still exists today. As of February 2020, the program had given more than 100,000 paid self-driving rides in Aptiv’s — now Motional’s — self-driving vehicles per the Lyft app.
 Aptiv’s investment in Las Vegas expanded as those ridership numbers grew. The company opened in December 2018 a 130,000-square-foot technical center in the city to house its fleet of autonomous vehicles, as well as an engineering team dedicated to research and development of software and hardware systems, validation and mapping.
 Motional describes today’s announcement as a “quantum leap” for the partnership. The robotaxi services will use next-generation vehicles based on a Hyundai vehicle platform. The vehicles will be integrated with sensors, computers and software to enable fully-driverless operation and remote vehicle assistance. Motional, which today uses BMW 5 Series and Chrysler Pacifica Hybrid minivans, said the fleet will also grow “significantly.”
 The companies didn’t provide details on which cities it will offer service in or just how big these fleets will be. Motional has testing operations in Boston, Las Vegas and Pittsburgh. Motional did indicate that this robotaxi service partnership with Lyft will extend beyond the initial launch cities. That doesn’t mean Lyft will be it’s only partner. Earlier this year, Motional struck a deal with on-demand shuttle company Via to launch a shared robotaxi service for the public in a U.S. city in the first half of 2021. The companies said the aim is to develop a “blueprint” for on-demand shared robotaxis and learn how these driverless vehicles can be integrated into mass transit.
 “This agreement is a testament to our global leadership in driverless technology. We’re at the frontier of transportation innovation, moving robotaxis from research to road,” Motional President and CEO Karl Iagnemma, said in a statement. “Our aim is to not only build safe, reliable, and accessible driverless vehicles, but to deliver them at significant scale. We’re partnering with Lyft to do exactly that.”
title: Motional和Lyft的目标是2023年在美国主要城市部署无人驾驶机器人服务
title_en: Motional and Lyft target 2023 to deploy driverless robotaxi services in major U.S. cities

note:
该投资来自比尔·盖茨（Bill Gates）支持的Breakthrough Energy Ventures和生态系统完整性基金（Ecosystem Integrity Fund），后者领导了该公司的最新一轮融资，此前的投资者包括Amazon Climate Pledge Fund，Horizo​​ns Ventures，Shell Ventures和Summa Equity。
 航空是导致全球变暖的碳排放的巨大贡献者，航空业和航运业将是最难脱碳的行业之一。电气化技术尚未解决推进飞机或移动大型航海船只的方式，技术专家之间正在形成共识，即氢将是确保零排放飞行成为现实的最佳解决方案。
  “从我的角度出发，我非常坚信氢，如果我有足够的零碳氢，而且它足够便宜，那么我什么都可以做，”执行董事兼科学负责人Breakthrough Energy Ventures的埃里克·托内（Eric Toone）说。
 业界支持者认为，ZeroAvia也可能是解决许多航空业问题的门票。该公司已经与英国航空公司建立了合作伙伴关系，并从英国政府获得了1630万美元的赠款，以确保其19座氢电动飞机到2023年能投入市场。
 长期投资者Shell Ventures在本轮融资中将对ZeroAvia的承诺加倍，这是由于该公司采用独特的方法来整合各种成熟技术，以将混合氢电飞行推向市场。
 “每个组件在某种意义上都不是唯一的。动力总成不是唯一的，燃油系统也不是唯一的……[但是]将所有这些整合到一个系统中，不仅是从技术上让飞机升空，而且还与监管机构就我们需要改变什么以及所有壳牌公司氢气副总裁保罗·博格斯（Paul Bogers）说。
 该公司表示，ZeroAvia现在正在开发和测试经过认证的ZA-600动力总成，该动力总成可以使10-20个座位的飞机飞行500英里。今年早些时候，该公司在9月完成了首次商业规模的电池电动飞行和氢燃料电池飞机的首次飞行。该公司表示，预计将在未来三个月内完成250英里的长途飞行。 
对于ZeroAvia的创始人兼首席执行官，连续企业家，其最后一家公司eMotorWerks被出售给Enel的Val Miftakhov而言，这次飞行证明了他的论点，即氢能是航空业的答案。
  “我们最近的里程碑式成就正在缩小航空业开始从化石燃料过渡的差距。实际上，现在有十多家具有前瞻性的航空公司正在加紧准备在2023年准备就绪时实施我们的动力总成。”米夫塔霍夫在一份声明中说。航空和金融市场都开始意识到氢是通往大规模零排放商业飞行的唯一有意义的途径。用氢气为100个座位的飞机供电并非不可能。我们非常感谢顶级投资者加入我们的激动人心的旅程的下一阶段；开创航空业的新黄金时代。”
 Miftakhov说，公司成功的关键在于认识到，利用现有的电解技术，天然气可以转化为氢气，从而可以为飞机系统提供动力。
 “我们管理燃料供应，”米夫塔霍夫说。 “我们已经在克兰菲尔德的机场做到了。我们用电和水自己做燃料。我们不会在制氢领域建立新技术。我们在电解方面利用合作伙伴。”
 该公司目前正与Enapter合作提供电解器，作为正在进行的演示的一部分，该公司希望在英国和美国的其他机场部署更大的系统。
 “消费规模使您可以经济地在现场制造燃料。这就是氢的游戏规则改变者。”米夫塔霍夫说。 “您不必运输它。您不必液化它。您不必担心运输它，因为它是一种低密度燃料。这很乱。如果您将其全部现场生产这些挑战消失了。我们认为基础架构会很好。”
 正是Miftakhov在建立eMotorWerks方面的经验使他探索了氢作为航空业的解决方案。他说：“电池的能量密度不足以满足飞机所需的能量。” “航空运输是最耗能的交通方式……您不能停留在中间。” 
米夫塔霍夫说，最初，该公司将为在亚洲和加勒比海地区短途飞行中使用的10至20座飞机的改装产品推向市场。
 但是，该公司与世界上七家最大的飞机制造商有合作关系，他们正在研究将公司的动力总成整合到飞机上的方法，而且公司正在逐步整合到A320或A320等大型客机上到本世纪末的737年。
 米夫塔霍夫说，向氢燃料车队过渡的过程将比新一代制造商的技术能力付出更多。它还将需要政府干预。
 “到2050年，每个人都希望实现零排放和净零排放。 [但是]我们已经来不及了。 2050年是一生的距离。政府将进来，并说我们将迫使加速汽车生产周转率。”他说。
 Miftakhov希望在该法规出台时做好准备。该公司已经与运营商签署了合作意向书，以改装100多架飞机。并为研发新型飞机打下了基础。
 对于像亚马逊这样致力于在2040年之前实现脱碳的企业投资者来说，创新和行业采用尚不能很快完成。
 全球可持续发展副总裁卡拉·赫斯特（Kara Hurst）表示：“亚马逊成立了气候承诺基金，以支持技术和服务的开发，这将使亚马逊和其他公司提前十年实现《巴黎协定》的目标，到2040年实现零碳净额。亚马逊“ ZeroAvia的零排放航空动力总成具有真正的潜力，可以帮助航空业脱碳，我们希望这项投资将进一步加快创新速度，以实现大规模零排放航空运输。”
note_en:
The investment came from the Bill Gates-backed   Breakthrough Energy Ventures  and the   Ecosystem Integrity Fund ,   which led the company’s latest round, alongside previous investors   Amazon Climate Pledge Fund,   Horizons Ventures ,   Shell Ventures,  and   Summa Equity .
 Aviation is a huge contributor to the carbon emissions that cause global warming, and the industry, along with shipping will be one of the hardest to decarbonize. Electrification technologies have yet to account for ways to propel aircraft or move massive seafaring vessels, and a consensus is emerging among technologists that Hydrogen will be the best solution to ensure zero emissions flight can become a reality.
  “ I’m a big believer in hydrogen from the perspective that if I have enough zero carbon hydrogen, and it’s cheap enough, then I can do anything ,” said Eric Toone, the executive managing director and science lead Breakthrough Energy Ventures.
 Industry backers think that ZeroAvia may also be the ticket to solving many of the aviation industry’s problems. The company has established a partnership with British Airways and received a $16.3 million grant from the UK government to ensure that its 19-seat hydrogen-electric airplane is ready for the market by 2023.
 Longtime investor Shell Ventures doubled down on its commitment to ZeroAvia with this round, on the back of the company’s unique approach to integrating various proven technologies to bring hybrid hydrogen-electric flight to market.
 “Each individual component is not unique in a sense. The powertrain is not unique, the fuel system isn’t unique… [But] bringing it all together in a system, is not just technically getting a plane in the sky but having those conversations with regulators about what do we need to change and all the steps involved in getting to a commercial play,” said Paul Bogers, the vice president of Hydrogen at Shell.
 ZeroAvia is now developing and testing the certification-ready ZA-600 powertrain, which can fly a 10-20 seat aircraft up to 500 miles, the company said. And earlier this year the company completed the first commercial-scale battery electric flight and the first flight of a hydrogen fuel cell powered aircraft in September. The company said it would expect to complete a long-distance flight of 250 miles within the next three months.
 For Val Miftakhov, the founder and chief executive of ZeroAvia and a serial entrepreneur whose last company, eMotorWerks, was sold to Enel, the flights prove out his thesis that hydrogen power is the answer for the aviation industry.
  “Our most recent milestone achievements are closing the gap for the airline industry to begin its transition away from fossil fuels. In fact, over ten forward-looking airlines are now gearing up to implement our powertrains when they are ready in 2023,” said Miftakhov said in a statement. “Both aviation and the financial markets are waking up to the idea that hydrogen is the only meaningful path towards large-scale, zero-emission commercial flight. Powering a 100-seat plane on hydrogen is not out of the question. We feel deeply grateful to our top-tier investors for joining us in the next phase of our exciting journey; to bring in a new golden age of aviation.”
 The key to the company’s success is the realization that using existing electrolyzer technologies, natural gas can be converted into hydrogen which can be used to power the aircraft’s systems, Miftakhov said.
 “We manage fuel supply,” Miftakhov said. “We’ve done that already at the airport in Cranfield. We make our own fuel from electricity and water.  We are not building new technology in the hydrogen manufacturing space. We’re utilizing partners on the electrolysis side.”
 The company is working with Enapter to provide the electrolyzer for now, and expects to deploy larger systems at other airfields in the UK and US as part of its ongoing demonstrations.
 “The scale of consumption allows you to economically make fuel on site. That’s the game changer for hydrogen,” Miftakhov said. “You don’t have to transport it.. You can’t liquefy it.. You don’t have to worry about transporting it because it’s a low density fuel.. It’s a mess.. If you make it on site all of those challenges go away.. We think infrastructure is going to be fine.”
 It was Miftakhov’s experience building eMotorWerks that led him to explore hydrogen as the solution for the aviation industry. “Batteries aren’t energy dense enough for the amount of energy you need for the aircraft,” he said. “Aviation is the most energy intensive form of transit… and you can’t stop in the middle.”
 Initially, the company will go to market with retrofits for the 10 to 20 seat aircraft in use on short haul flights across Asia and the Caribbean, Miftakhov said.
 However, the company has relationships with seven of the biggest aircraft manufacturers in the world who are working on ways to integrate the company’s powertrains into their aircraft, Miftakhov said, and the company is on track to integrate into large passenger planes like an A320 oer a 737 by the end of the decade.
 Transitioning to a hydrogen fleet is going to take more than the technical ability of a new breed of manufacturers though, Miftakhov said. It will also require government intervention.
 “By 2050 everybody wants to be zero emission and net zero. [But] we are already too late. 2050 is one vehicle lifetime away. The governments are going to come in and say we’re going to force the acceleration of the vehicle generation turnover,” he said.
 Miftakhov wants to be ready when that regulation happens. The company has signed letters of intent with operators to retrofit over 100 aircraft already. And has laid the groundwork for working on new types of aircrafts.
 For corporate investors like Amazon who have committed to decarbonize by 2040, the innovations and industry adoption can’t happen quickly enough.
 “Amazon created The Climate Pledge Fund to support the development of technologies and services that will enable Amazon and other companies to reach the goals of the Paris Agreement ten years early—achieving net zero carbon by 2040,” said Kara Hurst, VP Worldwide Sustainability, Amazon. “ZeroAvia’s zero-emission aviation powertrain has real potential to help decarbonize the aviation sector, and we hope this investment will further accelerate the pace of innovation to enable zero-emission air transport at scale.”
title: ZeroAvia的氢动力航空愿景从亚马逊，壳牌和比尔·盖茨（Bill Gates）支持的基金中获利2140万美元
title_en: ZeroAvia’s hydrogen-powered vision for aviation nets $21.4 million from Amazon, Shell, and Bill Gates-backed fund

note:
Gupta是一位连续企业家，其最后一家公司Pulse News在2013年被LinkedIn收购。Gupta在2016年（大约在被Microsoft收购时）离开LinkedIn，以寻求更有意义的东西。他决定寻求一种更好的方法来解决阿片类药物成瘾的流行病，该疾病在他离开LinkedIn的那一年导致了42,000多人死亡。
 根据美国卫生与公共服务部的数据，到2018年，过量使用导致的死亡人数将超过47,000。
  而且问题不会消失。 Bicycle Health的统计数据表明，有9200万美国人有发展为阿片类药物使用障碍的潜在风险，已有200万美国人被诊断为阿片类药物成瘾者。
  最初，该公司在加利福尼亚州红木城的一家诊所工作，但随着今年初COVID-19大流行在美国举行，并迫使治疗机构采取了预防措施以阻止这种疾病的传播，Bicycle Health开始了采用虚拟治疗方法。
 根据HHS颁布的新规定，许多只能亲自获得的疗法和药物治疗都可以远程分发。这一变化引起了远程护理服务的爆炸式增长，自行车健康公司（Bicycle Health）急于资本化。
 该公司目前在18个州提供治疗方案，并有望在2021年第一季度末在25个州提供治疗方案。
 支持这一增长的是由风险投资公司Signalfire领导的大量个人和机构投资者，Signalfire领导了Bicycle Health的种子轮融资。该公司总共从包括Signalfire，Hustle Fund，Romulus Capital在内的投资者以及像LinkedIn前首席执行官Jeff Weiner这样的个人投资者那里筹集了大约550万美元。 Sir Inkinen，Virta Health的创始人； Iora Health的创始人Rushika Fernandopulle；约翰·西蒙（John Simon）通过他的Greenlight基金共同创立了Catalyst。 
自行车健康部不仅将丁丙诺啡处方为药物，还提供了一组健康教练来解决排毒带来的行为和精神健康问题。
 Gupta说，该公司目前有三名健康教练来管理公司目前的2,000名患者的护理，这些教练得到了12名临床支持专家的支持。
 Gupta说，治疗是通过公司的移动应用程序进行交付和管理的，并通过定期的室内诊断测试来补充监测患者的病情。
 该公司与Goodrx合作，已经能够降低没有医疗保健的患者的非处方药成本，古普塔（Gupta）表示，Bicycle Health将与当地和国家的医疗保健提供商合作，以尽最大努力支付公司可以承担的费用。
 他说：“我们希望为患者提供尽可能多的费用补贴。”
 Gupta在今年早些时候接受TechCrunch采访时说，目标是“确保该行业确保他们正在帮助人们做出改变，而不是让他们陷入困境。”
 而且，尽管Gupta来自软件行业，其指导原则一直是快速行动并打破问题，但他意识到医疗保健并非如此。他说：“快速行动并打破事情是医疗保健的错误观念。” “您必须建立一个安全的空间……我的意思不是合法的安全空间，而是可以为患者提供服务的安全空间。”
note_en:
A serial entrepreneur whose last company, Pulse News, was acquired by  LinkedIn   back in 2013, Gupta left the LinkedIn in 2016 (around the time of the Microsoft acquisition) to pursue something more meaningful. He settled on trying to find a better way to address the opioid addiction epidemic, which was responsible for more than 42,000 deaths the year he left LinkedIn.
 By 2018 deaths from overdoses would exceed 47,000, according to data from the US Department of Health and Human Services.
  And the problem isn’t going away. Bicycle Health’s statistics indicate that 92 million Americans are potentially at risk for developing opioid use disorder and 2 million Americans are already diagnosed as opioid addicts.
  Initially, the company worked out of a clinic in Redwood City, Calif., but as the COVID-19 pandemic took hold in the US earlier this year and forced treatment facilities to undertake preventative measures to stop the spread of the disease, Bicycle Health began adopting virtual treatment methods.
 Under new regulations delivered by HHS, many therapies and drug treatments that had only been available in-person were able to be distributed remotely. The change caused an explosion in remote care services and companies like Bicycle Health rushed to capitalize.
 The company is currently offering treatment options in 18 states and is on track to be available in 25 states by the end of the first quarter of 2021.
 Backing that growth are a slew of individual and institutional investors led by the venture investment firm, Signalfire, which led Bicycle Health’s seed round. In all, the company has raised roughly $5.5 million from investors including Signalfire,  Hustle Fund,   Romulus Capital, and individual investors like Jeff Weiner, the former chief executive of LinkedIn; Sami Inkinen, the founder of Virta Health; Rushika Fernandopulle, the founder of Iora Health; and John Simon, the co-founder of General Catalyst through his Greenlight Fund.
 Bicycle Health both prescribes buprenorphine as a medical treatment and offers a team of health coaches to address the behavioral and mental health issues attendant with detoxifying.
 There are three health coaches on staff to manage the care of the company’s current roster of 2,000 patients and those coaches are bolstered by 12 clinical support specialists, Gupta said.
 Treatment is delivered and managed through the company’s mobile app, and is supplemented by regular in-home diagnostic testing to monitor a patient’s progress, according to Gupta.
 Working with Goodrx, the company has been able to drive down over-the-counter costs for patients who don’t have healthcare, and Gupta said that Bicycle Health would be working with local and national healthcare providers to try and defray as much of the costs as the company can.
 “We want to subsidize the costs as much as possible for our patient,” he said.
 The goal, Gupta said in an interview with TechCrunch earlier this year is “making sure that this industry is making sure that they’re helping people make a change instead of keeping them stuck.”
 And although Gupta comes from a software industry whose guiding principle has been to move fast and break things, he realizes that healthcare doesn’t work that way. “Move fast and break things is the wrong mindset for healthcare,” he said. “ You have to build a safe space … and i don’t mean a safe space legally but a safe space where patients can be served.”
title: 虚拟阿片类药物使用障碍疗法服务“自行车保健”即将在25个州推出

note_en:
This story is available exclusively to Business Insider subscribers.  Become an Insider and start reading now.
  TikTok routes the personal data of job applicants through servers in China, and only discloses this to candidates in certain countries, Business Insider has discovered.
  Some of the personal information TikTok says it collects about applicants is potentially highly sensitive, with the firm's own policies stating that it collects medical data; sex and race data; marital status; geolocation data, among many other categories.
 The revelation is an embarrassment for TikTok, which has spent much of 2020 maintaining that it is separate to its Chinese owner ByteDance, and fending off unproven insinuations by President Trump that it funnels user data to China.
 After being approached by Business Insider, TikTok said it would no longer store job applicant data in China.
  Until Wednesday, a simple URL on TikTok's careers page laid bare the company's connection to China explicitly.
 Applicants looking to fill the thousands of open positions across TikTok who visit the company's career page can complete and track their applications by logging into the site. Creating an account on the careers page using an email address or cell phone number keeps the users on  careers.tiktok.com, as does creating an account via LinkedIn.
 But electing to create an account using your Facebook login took users to an authorization dialogue box for "TikTok Career" that redirects to a Chinese-based website:  portal-tiktok.kundou.cn.
     The firm mentioned in that URL, Kundou, is  reportedly indirectly controlled by ByteDance, and based in Beijing. It provides support for backend software for recruitment within the company. It does not appear on ByteDance's  corporate structure diagram, which never mentions its Chinese operations.
 Business Insider understands all job applicants' data — not just those creating accounts with Facebook — is moved through Kundou's software during the process of applying for work at TikTok.
 "It's the classic example of being switched to a jurisdiction in the middle of a workflow without it being made crystal clear," says Alan Woodward, cybersecurity professor at the University of Surrey. "TikTok have been so clear about the [user] data for those outside China being held on servers in Singapore, yet they seem less obvious about the careers case."
 We found on Wednesday that TikTok had quietly removed the URL redirect after we flagged it. The firm declined to comment explicitly on the URL redirect when approached by Business Insider.
 It is not the only time TikTok gives away that it ships job applicants' data to China.
 TikTok also says in policy documents that it sends applicants' data to China — but it doesn't tell everyone
 TikTok offers job applicants different privacy policy documents depending on where they're based, and not all disclose that information goes to China.
 In the US, job applicants are told they will be providing data to ByteDance Inc (based in the Cayman Islands), TikTok Inc (based in the US), and a third company that does not appear on ByteDance's company organizational chart: Funnico Inc. Funnico Inc is a Delaware-based company, formerly known as Flipagram, which ByteDance acquired in 2016.
   In the  five-page document, no mention is made of sharing that data with China — despite anyone using a Facebook login to submit the application immediately submitting their details to a Chinese URL.
  In the UK version of the privacy policy for job applicants, the detail is more nuanced. In a short,  one-page version of the policy, TikTok states: "As you know, we are part of the global TikTok Group – and entities across the TikTok network are involved in processing data."
  A longer,  six-page version of the policy for UK applicants says: "TikTok UK's headquarters is located in China and your data is stored in China, as part of our applicant tracking system."
  The claim that TikTok's UK headquarters are located in China appears to contradict information on Companies House, the UK's register of companies, and ByteDance's own organizational chart. Since it was incorporated on May 6, 2016, TikTok Information Technologies UK, which trades as TikTok UK, has always been based in offices in London — most recently in a WeWork.
 ByteDance's organizational chart, which does not include all the companies it owns because of the scale of acquisitions made, also suggests that TikTok Information Technologies UK Ltd feeds into TikTok Ltd in the Cayman Islands, which is run under the umbrella of ByteDance Ltd, also in the Cayman Islands. China is never mentioned.
 Business Insider understands the mention of TikTok UK being headquartered in China is an error, though TikTok could not provide an explanation of how the claim came to be included in its privacy policy.
 Other European countries' privacy policies, in French and German, as well as those for people applying for jobs in Japan and Singapore, mention China. Like the US, Malaysia's privacy policy for job applicants doesn't mention China.
   That's something Woodward worries about. "Once your data is within China it is subject to their laws and those are very different to those people in EU might be used to," he said. "You have to be aware that China is an authoritarian regime and laws such as their Intelligence Act are very broad. If you're dealing with any company that is ultimately controlled from China you need to keep an eye on where your data is being sent."
 TikTok did not deny that it failed to disclose the fact it sends job applicant data to China, and was unable to explain how the UK's privacy policy said it was headquartered in China.
 The spokesperson said: "We are aware of a number of inaccuracies and outdated references in our privacy policies that apply to our recruitment process, and we are in the process of updating these."
 They added: "To be crystal clear, TikTok does not have a headquarters, nor has it ever been headquartered in China. TikTok is not available in China. All TikTok user data is stored in the US and Singapore. We are also establishing a new data centre in Ireland by 2022, which will store UK and European user data."
 The firm is also altering its processes so job candidate data is no longer stored in China.
 TikTok has always highlighted that its parent firm is based in the Cayman Islands, not China
 There is no evidence that TikTok sends app user data to China, but the revelation on jobs data is embarrassing as the firm fends off political scrutiny in the US and Europe over its Chinese ownership. The firm employs around 1,600 workers in Europe, and around 1,500 in the US.
 The company has come in for scrutiny over its handling of user data. In February 2019, TikTok was fined as part of a settlement after its earlier US incarnation Musical.ly was accused of illegally collecting data on children. It was subsequently accused of  breaking the terms of that settlement, and continuing to collect data on minors.
 The firm  continues to fight a prospective ban in the US and has repeatedly denied claims it spies on behalf of China.
   TikTok's immediate parent company is based in the Cayman Islands, and the firm says it is based in a country known for providing tax relief to businesses rather than being based in China. Parent company ByteDance's main offices are in Beijing, with others spread across the country.
 The company has said  in front of UK and Australian parliamentary commissions that it does not transfer Western users' data to China, and limits employee access to that data.

After testing investor patience for three weeks, bitcoin has finally crossed above $20,000 to reach fresh all-time highs.
 The number one cryptocurrency by market value jumped over the key psychological threshold during the early U.S. trading hours, surpassing the previous peak price of $19,920 recorded on Dec. 1. At the current price of $20,374,  bitcoin is up 5.4% over 24 hours, according to CoinDesk’s Bitcoin Price Index (BPI).
 Bitcoin’s value has doubled in the past three months and the institutional-led rally  looks sustainable. Meanwhile, other prominent cryptocurrencies such as  ethereum,  litecoin and  XRP are still down 58% to 88% from their respective lifetime highs reached three years ago.

Zoomin是一个所谓的“知识编排”平台，可帮助用户从企业文档中提取答案，今天透露，该公司已从包括Salesforce Ventures，Bessemer Venture Partners和Viola Growth在内的投资者那里筹集了2100万美元。这项投资在今天之前尚未披露，从2018年开始分几批支付。
 Zoomin成立于2015年，在纽约和特拉维夫均设有枢纽，旨在帮助企业更轻松地找到更实用的大量技术内容。公司可能有成千上万的手册，指南，培训用具，在线社区讨论等，但是所有这些完全不同的内容通常是由不同的团队，人员和系统创建和管理的，并且经常存在于孤岛中。 Zoomin首席执行官兼联合创始人Gal Oron表示，Zoomin“统一”了此内容，并以“更直观，更个性化的方式”提供了该内容。
   Zoomin的产品也许可以粗略地描述为企业产品内容的白标搜索引擎，尽管Oron认为传统的联合搜索解决方案着重于对内容建立索引，并将用户从他们的搜索点带到包含结果的任何外部渠道。另一方面，Zoomin可以在用户进行搜索的任何地方（无论公司部署了Zoomin技术的任何地方）将答案带给用户。
 Oron告诉VentureBeat：“这意味着他们无需在不同的站点之间导航，也不必经历这种自然而然地伴随着“上下文切换”的支离破碎。
 Zoomin的使用方式很大程度上取决于客户对其的需求。它可能是一个独立的技术资源中心，也许类似于公司范围内的Intranet或什至是公共门户网站，它可以将完全不同的静态内容转换为充满了过滤器，自动建议，建议等内容的动态搜索界面。也可以是一个小部件，提供与给定情况的上下文相关的内容，并烘焙到客户自己的应用程序中，例如客户关系管理（CRM）工具。
 “在某些情况下，客户用Zoomin替换了现有的门户，在其他情况下，他们保留了门户，但使用Zoomin来创建增强的，直观的，个性化的体验，” Oron补充说。
  Zoomin附带了各种集成选项，包括REST API，Javascript API和命令行界面（CLI），同时它提供了可以下载，定制和与Salesforce或ServiceNow集成的预构建应用程序。 
在引擎盖下，Zoomin表示，它使用内部开发和培训的监督和无监督机器学习（ML）模型，以及现成的ML服务。
 “ Zoomin的知识图将企业内容，用户和交互联系在一起，为平台的文本分析和分类，动态排名，内容推荐和预测见解提供了动力，” Oron解释说。
 Analytics（分析）在Zoomin的产品中也发挥了相当大的作用，包括“流量分析”，其中详细说明了流量来自何处（包括引荐域和位置）； “内容洞察力”，它可以显示哪些主题和出版物获得最多的参与度；和“搜索见解”，这为公司提供了可用于调整UX的搜索模式数据。
 “这些见解旨在帮助我们的客户了解用户正在搜索的内容，了解哪些搜索字词没有任何结果，分析搜索过滤器的使用情况等等，” Oron补充说。
  尽管Zoomin的运作相当不错，但它已经吸引了许多知名客户，包括现在Adobe拥有的Workfront，中国酒店业巨头Shiji和网络安全资深人士Imperva。
 Zoomin完全被启动，直到Bessemer在2018年进行了首笔投资，随后是Salesforce Ventures在2019年的后续投资。两家风投公司都在今年与以色列的Viola Growth一起对这家初创公司进行了再投资。
note_en:
Zoomin, a so-called “knowledge orchestration” platform that helps users extract answers from enterprise documentation, today revealed that it has raised $21 million from investors including Salesforce Ventures, Bessemer Venture Partners, and Viola Growth. The investment, which has been undisclosed before today, came in several tranches starting in 2018.
 Founded in 2015, Zoomin, which has hubs in New York and Tel Aviv, is setting out to help businesses make their vast pools of technical content easier to find and more usable. Companies may have many thousands of manuals, guides, training paraphernalia, online community discussions, and more, but all this disparate content is typically created and managed by different teams, people, and systems, and often exists in silos. Zoomin “unifies” this content, and delivers it in a more “intuitive and personalized way,” according to Zoomin CEO and cofounder Gal Oron.
   Zoomin’s product can perhaps be crudely described as a white-label search engine for enterprise product content, though Oron argues that traditional federated search solutions focus on indexing content and taking users from their point of search to whatever external channel contains the results. Zoomin, on the other hand, brings the answers to the user wherever it is they are conducting the search — wherever a company has deployed Zoomin’s technology.
 “This means they don’t need to navigate across different sites and experience the fragmentation and drop-off that naturally accompanies this kind of ‘context switching,’ Oron told VentureBeat.
 How Zoomin is used pretty much depends on what the customer needs from it. It could be a standalone  technical resource center, perhaps something akin to a company-wide intranet or even a public portal, transforming disparate static content into a dynamic search interface replete with filters, auto-suggestions, recommendations, and more. Or it could be a widget that offers content relevant to the context of a given situation,  baked into the customer’s own applications such as a customer relationship management (CRM) tool.
 “In some cases, customers replace their existing portals with Zoomin, in other cases they keep their portal but use Zoomin to create an enhanced, intuitive, personalized experience,” Oron added.
  Zoomin ships with various integration options, including REST APIs, Javascript APIs, and command line interfaces (CLIs), while it offers pre-built apps that can be downloaded, customized, and integrated  with Salesforce or  ServiceNow.
  Under the hood, Zoomin says that it uses both supervised and unsupervised machine learning (ML) models, developed and trained in-house, alongside off-the-shelf ML services.
 “Zoomin’s knowledge graph ties together enterprise content, users and interactions, powering the platform’s text analysis and classification, dynamic ranking, content recommendations and predictive insights,” Oron explained.
 Analytics also play a sizable part in Zoomin’s offering, including “traffic insights,” which details where traffic is coming from (including the referring domain and location); “content insights,” which surfaces which topics and publications receive the most engagement; and “search insights,” which gives companies search pattern data which can be used to tweak the UX.
 “These insights are designed to help our customers understand what users are searching for, learn which search terms are yielding no results, analyze the usage of search filters, and more,” Oron added.
  Although Zoomin has operated fairly under the radar, it has amassed a number of notable clients including now  Adobe-owned Workfront, Chinese hospitality giant  Shiji, and  cybersecurity veteran Imperva.
 Zoomin was entirely bootstrapped up until Bessemer’s inaugural investment in 2018, which was followed by Salesforce Ventures follow-on investment in 2019. Both VC firms re-invested in the startup this year, alongside Israel’s Viola Growth.
title: Zoomin将公司的手册和其他技术文档统一在一起，以使其员工和客户更容易使用，并筹集了2100万美元
title_en: Zoomin, which unifies the manuals and other technical documentation of companies to make them more accessible to their employees and customers, raises $21M

Vercel为构建前端应用程序提供了平台，今天宣布又获得了4000万美元的B轮融资，使其总融资额达到6100万美元。这笔资金将主要用于扩展应用程序开发平台的范围，该平台旨在简化使用Next.js构建应用程序的工作。Next.js是一个框架，该框架基于JavaScript的实现（称为React），最初由Facebook开创。
 最新一轮融资由GV牵头，新投资者Greenoaks Capital，Bedrock Capital和Geodesic Capital参与了该项目。现有投资者包括Accel Partners和CRV。
  基于Next.js的Vercel平台为开发人员提供了预览功能，使您可以在部署之前直观地检查存储库中的Web应用程序和网站的前端。 Vercel首席执行官Guillermo Rauch说，Vercel将扩展该功能，使最终用户能够在部署Web应用程序和页面之前检查它们。
 Rauch说，这种功能将使开发人员和最终用户更容易就数字业务转型计划进行协作，而这些计划通常涉及使Web应用程序现代化。他补充说：“我们希望实现跨多个角色的协作。”
 Vercel正在提出将应用程序前端开发与后端分离的理由，作为加速应用程序开发的一部分。大多数应用程序的后端很少更改，而最终用户应用程序体验则需要不断发展和改进。
 将应用程序的前端开发与后端分开，可以更轻松地更新应用程序，同时继续通过应用程序编程接口（API）访问各种后端应用程序。
 Rauch说，这种核心功能是从Airbnb到TripAdvisor的组织现在都使用Vercel平台构建其Web应用程序前端的主要原因。寻找和留住了解JavaScript的开发人员比雇用具有Java技能的开发人员要容易得多。实际上，尽管大多数后端服务仍是用Java开发的，但是Web应用程序的前端已经大多使用基于某种形式的JavaScript的框架来构建。 
基于JavaScript的React框架的过渡也正在加速，因为Google调整了其页面排名系统，以包括根据其Google Web Vitals得分的网站或应用程序的性能。应用程序或网站的速度越慢，排名就越低，这反过来又会影响电子商务网站针对特定搜索词的排名情况。
 Vercel平台形式的Next.js不仅使组织能够构建和部署更具吸引力的Web应用程序，还使他们能够部署比使用旧版Web应用程序框架构建的应用程序快得多的应用程序。
 当然，Next.js并不是构建现代Web应用程序的唯一方法。例如，一些组织喜欢Gatsby，这是一个基于开源Angular框架构建React应用程序的框架。
 无论采用哪种框架，在COVID-19大流行带来的经济衰退之后，Web和移动应用程序的现代化速度都在加快。各种规模的组织都发现有必要更积极地在线吸引用户，只是为了在个人仍在家中大量工作以抵御大流行的蔓延之际保持联系。
 GV的普通合伙人泰森·克拉克（Tyson Clark）表示，这家风险投资公司之所以被Vercel吸引，是因为自JAMstack的概念开始在Web开发人员中吸引人以来，该公司一直在寻求投资应用程序开发初创公司。 （JAMstack是使用JavaScript，API和标记语言来加速Web应用程序开发的首字母缩写。）
 Clark表示，Vercel通过更轻松地结合使用无服务器计算功能和无头后端服务来构建Web应用程序，将这一概念提升到了一个新的水平。克拉克补充说：“我们看好无服务器。”
 克拉克说，理想情况下，主要投资者希望看到Vercel成为一家上市公司，但他还指出，在一个拥挤的应用程序开发领域中，该部门不太可能维持超过两家上市公司。 
目前尚不清楚目前在影响Web应用程序部署的内容交付网络（CDN）提供商在多大程度上会响应新兴企业的兴起，从而使构建这些应用程序变得更加容易。 他们可以选择构建开放源代码框架的实现，也可以选择收购诸如Vercel之类的供应商。  无论选择哪种方式，很明显，专注于前端应用程序开发的平台所推动的创新速度正在加快。 现在的问题不仅仅在于是否接受React框架来构建这些应用程序，而是问题的程度和程度。
note_en:
Vercel, which provides a platform for building front-end applications, today announced an additional $40 million in series B funding, bringing its total raised to $61 million. The funding will be primarily used to expand the reach of an application development platform designed to make it simpler to build applications using Next.js, a framework based on an implementation of JavaScript known as React that was originally pioneered by Facebook.
 This latest round of funding was led by GV, with participation from new investors Greenoaks Capital, Bedrock Capital, and Geodesic Capital. Existing investors include Accel Partners and CRV.
  The Vercel platform, based on Next.js, provides developers with a preview capability that makes it possible to visually inspect the front end of web applications and websites within a repository before they are deployed. Vercel will be extending that capability to enable end users to inspect web applications and pages before they are deployed, Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch said.
 That capability will make it easier for developers and end users to collaborate on digital business transformation initiatives that most often involve modernizing web applications, Rauch said. He added “We want to enable collaboration across multiple personas.”
 Vercel is making a case for separating the development of the front end of applications from the back end as part of an effort to accelerate application development. The back end of most applications rarely changes, while the end user application experience needs to constantly evolve and improve.
 Separating the front-end development of an application from the back end makes it easier to update applications while continuing to access myriad backend applications via application programming interfaces (APIs).
 That core capability is the primary reason organizations ranging from Airbnb to TripAdvisor now build the front end of their web applications using the Vercel platform, Rauch said. It’s a lot easier to find and retain developers that understand JavaScript than it is to hire developers with, for example, Java skills. In fact, while most back-end services are still developed in Java, the front ends of web applications are already mostly built using frameworks based on some form of JavaScript.
 The transition to the React framework based on JavaScript is also accelerating because Google has adjusted its page ranking system to include how well a website or application performs according to its  Google Web Vitals score. The slower the application or website, the lower the ranking, which in turn affects things like how well an ecommerce site might rank for a specific search term.
 Next.js in the form of the Vercel platform not only enables organizations to build and deploy web applications that are more engaging, it also enables them to deploy applications that are significantly faster than those built using legacy web application frameworks.
 Of course, Next.js is not the only approach to building a modern web application. For example, some organizations favor Gatsby, a framework for building React applications based on the open source Angular framework.
 Regardless of the framework employed, the rate at which web and mobile applications are being modernized is accelerating in the wake of the economic downturn brought on by the COVID-19 pandemic. Organizations of all sizes are discovering the need to engage users more proactively online simply to stay relevant at a time when individuals are still largely working from home to combat the spread of the pandemic.
 GV general partner Tyson Clark said the venture capital firm was attracted to Vercel because it has been looking to invest in an application development startup since the concept of JAMstack began to gain traction among web developers. (JAMstack is an acronym for employing JavaScript, APIs, and markup languages to accelerate the development of web applications.)
 Vercel takes that concept to the next level by making it easier to build web applications using a combination of serverless computing functions and headless backend services, Clark said, adding “We’re bullish on serverless.”
 Clark said that ideally the lead investors would like to see Vercel emerge as a public company, but he also noted that it’s unlikely this sector of a crowded application development field will be able to sustain more than two public companies.
 It’s also unclear to what degree providers of content delivery networks (CDNs) that currently factor in the deployment of web applications will respond to the rise of startups that make it easier to build these applications. They may opt to build their implementations of open source frameworks or acquire a vendor such as Vercel.
 Regardless of the path chosen, it’s clear the rate of innovation enabled by platforms that focus on the development of front-end applications is accelerating. The issue now isn’t so much whether to embrace React frameworks for building these applications as it is to what degree and how soon.

虽然我们是第一个报告SolarWinds安全漏洞的人，但该漏洞可能已经暴露了他们的Downloads FTP服务器凭据，从而使攻击者能够推送恶意二进制文件并攻击美国政府和SolarWinds＆＃39;。从那时起，其他一些知名客户，就出现了有关SolarWinds安全漏洞的更多信息，从而可以更深入地了解可能暴露的内容以及它是否可能导致大规模违反美国政府的行为。尽管大多数安全研究人员认为这不是造成漏洞的主要原因，并且针对SolarWinds进行了复杂而复杂的供应链攻击，但我们认为这些小的安全漏洞可能会使攻击者获得更大的收益。攻击面来进行攻击，最终可能有助于巩固他们在SolarWinds基础架构中的立足点，以进行侦察和逃避检测。
  安全研究员Vinoth Kumar告诉我们，自2018年以来，SolarWinds可能一直在向下载服务器公开FTP凭据。为了证实他的主张，Vinoth共享了以下链接，该链接指向在mib-importer GitHub存储库中公开的，可能属于SolarWinds员工的暴露配置文件，网址为https://github.com/xkozus00/mib-importer/blob/master /Src/Lib/PurgeApp/PurgeApp.exe.config，他进一步补充说，在向Web存档提供回购基本URL时，它表明Web存档最早是在2018年6月存档该页面的，这是最后一次页面已存档。因此，我们得出结论，直到Vinoth向SolarWinds PSIRT报告该公开存储库中的FTP服务器凭据和该潜在存储库中的其他潜在敏感信息之前，它在公共领域的存在时间可能已超过1年。
   这表明，SolarWinds可能早就暴露了敏感的内部凭据，直到引起注意为止，这反过来可能使攻击者有机会窃取有关SolarWinds的证书和其他有价值的内部信息来执行大型使用后门的SolarWinds Orion软件对美国政府和其他顶级组织进行大规模攻击。
  “ mib进口商”安全研究人员于2019年11月在GitHub上找到了公共GitHub存储库，该存储库可能属于SolarWinds员工，其中暴露了秘密（例如FTP用户名和密码），据说该存储库于2018年6月左右存在。
 经过分析，SaveBreach团队发现SolarWinds Orion允许用户将MIB文件导入其中。 MIB文件用于监视网络设备。显然，mib-importer工具是由SolarWinds开发的，用于将MIB文件导入Orion。我们从SolarWinds文档页面中找到了有关导入MIB文件的以下数据（参考-1，2，3）
  管理信息库（MIB）是一种结构，用于描述设备可以报告的所有对象，例如CPU，风扇或温度。 MIB包含名称，数据类型和对象标识符（OID）。 MIB是一种层次结构，显示为导航树。 MIB树中的每个条目都是特定设备上特定组件的值。
 SolarWinds维护一个MIB数据库，该数据库充当用于监视各种网络设备的OID的存储库。 MIB数据库会定期更新。 
从我们最近与Vinoth的对话中可以看出，关于SolarWinds的凭据和可能更敏感的数据在公开域中已经存在了很长一段时间，直到最终被删除。 Vinoth怀疑数据中可能还包含证书，而不仅仅是FTP凭据，仅凭这些凭据就足以对恶意二进制文件进行签名，然后将它们作为合法软件传递出去而上传到FTP服务器。
 问：从什么时候开始您就可以知道GitHub仓库了？您是否认为攻击者可以在将近3年的时间里对他们的基础结构进行持久化来实施攻击？
 Vinoth：我不确定，即使在2018年6月，该仓库中也有40次提交。存档中只有一页找不到其他内容。
 问：攻击者已将签名的二进制文件放在下载服务器上。攻击者获得对文件上传服务器的访问权限以对二进制文件进行签名后，可能会遵循什么过程？
 Vinoth：不确定，但是我可能会错过检查可能包含证书的回购协议。
 Vinoth在推特上说，自2018年6月17日起，GitHub存储库已向公众开放
   这就提出了许多问题。是用于对从该公共GitHub存储库或从公开泄漏的任何其他信息中获得的二进制文件进行签名的证书。暴露的证书可能已使黑客能够对其恶意的SolarWinds Orion二进制文件进行签名，并将其作为由SolarWinds开发的合法软件进行传播，随后将它们使用先前发现的泄漏的FTP凭据上传到“下载”服务器。 
我们可以得出部分结论，即GitHub上公开的内部信息存在了足够长的时间，攻击者已经利用它们获得了最初的立足点。 尽管目前还不清楚，因为FireEye和安全研究人员声称可能存在使用逃避技术的更复杂复杂的攻击链，但是我们确实认为这可能是SolarWinds漏洞和针对美国的广泛网络攻击的先兆 政府。
note_en:
While  we were the first to report on the SolarWinds security vulnerability that possibly could have exposed their Downloads FTP server credentials letting attackers to push malicious binaries and attack the US government and  SolarWinds' other high profile clients, some more information has surfaced regarding the SolarWinds security vulnerability since then, that gives more insight into what possibly was exposed and whether it could have led to this massive breach of the US government. While majority of security researchers are of the opinion that this wasn't the main reason of the breach, and that there was a complex and sophisticated supply chain attack targeting  SolarWinds, we believe  these small security lapses could have given the attackers a larger attack surface to carry out their attacks and eventually might have helped strengthen their foothold into the SolarWinds infrastructure, to perform reconnaissance and evade detection.
  Security researcher Vinoth Kumar, told us that "SolarWinds had been possibly exposing the FTP credentials to the Download server since at least 2018". To corroborate his claim, Vinoth shared the the following link to the Configuration file exposed that was exposed in the mib-importer GitHub repo possibly belonging to a  SolarWinds employee,  https://github.com/xkozus00/mib-importer/blob/master/Src/Lib/PurgeApp/PurgeApp.exe.config and he further added that, upon supplying the  repo base url to the Web Archive, it shows Web Archive had first archived the page back in June 2018, and that was the last time the page was archived. So we concluded that the credentials to the FTP server and other potentially sensitive information in that exposed repository possibly existed for more than 1 year in the public domain until Vinoth reported it to the SolarWinds PSIRT.
   This shows that SolarWinds might have been exposing their sensitive internal credentials since a fairly long time before it was brought to their notice, which in turn might have given its attackers an opportunity to steal certificates and other valuable internal information about SolarWinds to carry out the large scale attack against US government and other top organizations using the backdoored SolarWinds Orion software.
  A "mib-importer" public GitHub repository, possibly belonging to a SolarWinds employee with secrets (like FTP username and password) exposed, was found on GitHub by the security researcher in November 2019, which is said to have existed from around June 2018
 Upon analyzing, the SaveBreach team found out that SolarWinds Orion lets users import MIB files into it. MIB files are used for monitoring network devices. Apparently, the mib-importer tool was developed by SolarWinds to import MIB files into Orion. We found the following data from the   SolarWinds documentation pages regarding importing  MIB files (Reference –  1,  2,  3)
  Management Information Base (MIB) is a structure that describes all objects a device can report on, such as CPU, fan, or temperature. MIB contains the name, datatype, and the object identifier (OID). MIB is a hierarchical structure, displayed as a navigation tree. Every entry in the MIB tree is a value for a specific component on a specific device.
 SolarWinds maintains a MIB database that serves as a repository for the OIDs used to monitor a wide variety of network devices. The MIB database is updated regularly.
  From our most recent conversation with Vinoth, it appears that the credentials and possibly more sensitive data about SolarWinds was lying in public domain for a long time before finally being taken down. Vinoth doubts that the data might have also included certificates and not just FTP credentials, which was alone sufficient to sign the malicious binaries and upload them to the FTP server while passing off as legitimate software.
 Q: Since when do you think the GitHub repo might have been exposed? Do you think the attackers could have gained persistence into their infrastructure for almost 3 years to carry out the attack?
 Vinoth: I’m not sure, even on June 2018, 40 commits were there (in that repo). There was only one page available on archive couldn’t find anything else.
 Q: The Attackers had put signed binaries on the Download server. What was the process the attackers might have followed after getting access to the file upload server to sign the binaries?
 Vinoth: Not sure, but I could have missed checking the repo which could have had the certificate in it.
 Vinoth had tweeted that the GitHub repo was open to public since 17th June, 2018
   This raises many questions. Were the certificates used to sign the binaries obtained from that public GitHub repository or, from any other information leaked publicly.  Exposed certificate could have allowed hackers to sign their malicious SolarWinds Orion binaries and pass them off as legitimate software developed by SolarWinds, subsequently uploading them to the Downloads server with the previously found leaked FTP credentials.
  We can come to a partial conclusion that the internal information exposed on GitHub was there for a sufficiently long time for the attackers to have already exploited them to gain their initial foothold. Although unclear at this point, as there maybe a more sophisticated and complex attack chain with evasion techniques as being claimed by FireEye and security researchers, but we do feel this might have been a precursor to the SolarWinds breach and the widespread cyber attack against the US Government.
title: 自2018年以来，SolarWinds通过GitHub Repo“ mib-importer”泄露了FTP密码
title_en: SolarWinds Leaked FTP Password through GitHub Repo “mib-importer” since 2018

今天的比特币价格为20,416美元，24小时交易量为31,071,265,980美元。 BTC价格在过去24小时内上涨了5.7％。它的循环供应量为1900万BTC硬币，最大供应量为2100万。 Binance是当前最活跃的市场交易。为了探索地址和交易，您可以使用诸如blockchair.com，btc.com和btc.tokenview.com之类的区块浏览器。可以在http://www.bitcoin.org上找到更多信息。
   比特币是一种加密货币。它是一种基于密码学的去中心化数字货币。这样，它可以在不需要中央机构（例如中央银行或公司）的情况下运行。比特币不同于政府发行的或法定货币，例如美元或欧元，它们由该国中央银行控制。分散的性质使其可以在对等网络上运行，从而用户无需经过中介就可以相互进行资金汇兑。
  比特币是由一个不知名的个人或团体创建的，其名字叫中本聪（Satoshi Nakamoto），其构想是在白皮书中写明一种电子对等现金系统。直到今天，关于中本聪可能是谁的猜测和谣言仍未得到证实。
  比特币于2009年1月推出，第一个创世块于2009年1月9日开采。
   虽然公众将比特币视为某种具有物理外观的代币，但实际上却远非如此。在幕后，比特币实际上是一种分布式会计分类帐，以区块链的形式存储，因此命名为区块链。
 在像商业银行那样的集中式系统中，鉴于爱丽丝想与鲍勃进行交易的情况，该银行是唯一拥有描述账簿的实体，该账簿描述了爱丽丝和鲍勃的余额。当银行维护分类帐时，他们将对Alice是否有足够的资金发送给Bob进行验证。最后，交易成功完成后，银行会扣除爱丽丝的帐户，并以最新的金额记入鲍勃的帐户。
 相反，比特币以分散的方式工作。由于没有像银行这样的中心人物来验证交易和维护分类帐，因此分类帐的副本分布在整个比特币节点上。节点是任何人都可以下载并运行以参与网络的软件。这样，每个人都有一份爱丽丝和鲍勃拥有的余额的副本，并且不会有资金余额争议。 
现在，如果爱丽丝要使用比特币与鲍勃进行交易。爱丽丝将不得不向网络广播她的交易，她打算向鲍勃发送1美元的等值比特币。系统将如何确定她有足够的比特币来执行交易，并确保她不会两次花相同的金额。
 这里是采矿的地方。比特币矿工将使用他或她的计算机设备来验证要添加到分类账中的爱丽丝交易。为了阻止矿工添加任何任意交易，他们将需要解决一个复杂的难题。只有当矿工能够解决随机发生的难题（称为工作量证明）时，他或她才能够将交易添加到分类账中，并且记录是最终的。
 由于运行这些计算机设备会因购买设备而产生的资本支出和电力成本产生成本，因此矿工将获得新的比特币供应（这是其货币系统的一部分）以及希望进行交易的人支付的一定费用的奖励（在这种情况下是爱丽丝）。
 这使比特币分类账能够以一种不信任的方式抵御欺诈。虽然它具有弹性，但仍存在与系统相关的一些风险，例如51％的攻击，矿工控制着51％的总计算能力，并且还存在比特币协议无法控制的安全风险。
  如果您要保留一些比特币，重要的是要学习如何保护它们免受盗窃。
 在进行比特币交易时，通常会在个人计算机上进行。由于您的个人计算机已连接到Internet，因此有可能被恶意软件或间谍软件感染，从而可能危害您的比特币。
 减轻这种风险的方法之一是使用硬件钱包，例如Trezor和Ledger。硬件钱包本质上是类似于USB记忆棒的外部设备。硬件钱包可保护您的私钥，该私钥将比特币保存到个人计算机之外的外部设备中。当您打算进行交易时，您可以将硬件钱包连接到个人计算机中，并且进行交易的所有密钥签名都将在计算机外部的硬件中完成。 
但是，如果您在没有关键词备份的情况下物理上失去了硬件钱包，那么就没有其他方法可以恢复比特币了。这样，在设置硬件钱包时，请始终记住保留一个关键短语的副本，并将其放置在防火或防洪的地方。
  比特币减半或有时也称为减半，是指对矿工的区块奖励减少了一半。这是比特币货币政策的一部分，在该政策中，每隔大约4年，采矿奖励将减少一半，以限制2100万比特币的有限供应。一旦铸造了2100万比特币，矿工将不再获得新的比特币奖励，矿工有望通过交易费赚取收入。
 为了实时了解减半发生的实时时间，您可以在CoinGecko的比特币减半页面中添加书签。
 出于几个原因，这被视为重大事件。首先，交易者可能会猜测比特币的稀缺性会导致高波动性。其次，作为矿工奖励将减少，我们可能会看到一些矿工退出市场，因为他们无法维持较低的盈利能力。反过来，这可能会导致哈希率降低，并且采矿池可能会合并。因此，在减半期间，比特币网络可能会有些不稳定。
  我们不提供投资建议。比特币的价格从零开始，一直上升到您今天看到的市场价格。似乎由于以下原因，市场正在为比特币赋予价值。
 数字黄金-由于其数字稀缺性，它是可行的数字价值存储
  投机-这可能是由于市场效率低下，但有人猜测比特币可能是未来的资产类别 
话虽这么说，比特币也有风险。为了自己辨别比特币是否是一项好投资，重要的是要了解风险，并且只投资自己愿意损失的金额。
 比特币价格可能会为零-如果项目失败，软件错误或存在更新的更具创新性的数字货币将取代比特币。如果回想一下，2017年12月16日比特币的价值接近20,000美元。但在2018年12月17日，比特币的价格低至约3200美元。比特币是一种高度波动的资产类别，需要很高的风险偏好。
 尽管比特币是一种数字黄金，但它已经存在约十年了。与黄金相比，黄金已经有数百年的历史了。
  是的，随着比特币变得越来越被广泛采用，正在推出各种衍生产品，使您可以卖空比特币。如果您是机构投资者，CME和Bakkt提供受监管的比特币期货产品，您可以参与多头或空头比特币。另外，还有许多其他加密货币衍生品交易所，例如BitMEX，Binance期货，FTX，Deribit等。这些衍生品交易所没有受到正式监管，甚至可以提供高达100倍的杠杆。衍生合约是高风险的产品，您可能想在参与之前了解自己在做什么。
